response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with deep experience in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and full-stack development, but the role's core mandatory requirements are not met: professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments (2014–2015) rather than the required 5–10 years on Windows, there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, and there is no Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain background — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets systems work.
strengths: Solid low-latency and systems programming experience (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput microservices)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, open-source contributions and test tooling
Broad technology range with proven ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, far below the mandatory 5–10 years
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital markets / investment banking domain exposure
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; recent work is JavaScript/TypeScript-centric

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
